c74object.name: dk.sampler~
c74object.description: The dk.sampler~ object is an all-in-one playback object that allows for mono or stereo playback, optional loudness and spectral compensation, along with various sample playback controls and features.
c74object.discussion: You can instantiate dk.sampler~ (and any other UI objects) from the sidebar directly.

outlet.description: Audio output (right). The right outlet will be the same as the left outlet if the entry in the corpus is mono. It will only play back in true stereo when the source file is in stereo.

method.name: bang
method.description: Play a randomly selected sample from the loaded samples.

method.name: jump
method.digest: Jump to that position in the last played sample
method.description: Jump to that position in the last played sample (0.0 to 1.0).

method.name: list
method.digest: Play back a sample based on list message and contents
method.description:
If you send a single int it will play that sample from the polybuffer~ (counting from 1).
If you send a single float between 0. and 1. it will select a sample from the polybuffer~ based on the amount of samples loaded (e.g. 0. = the first sample, 1. = the last sample).
If you send a two item list the first number will be treated as sample selection and the second number will be treated as the playback speed of the sample where 60 = untransposed unless otherwise set by midinote.
If you send a three item list the the first two numbers are treated as mentioned above and the third item will be treated as the playback volume of the same. This can be a float from 0. to 1. or a MIDI value (int) from 0 to 127.
If you connect the output of dk.descriptors~ / dk.descriptorframe or dk.controllers it will select a random sample and use the loudness from the incoming descriptors to adjust playback loudness.
You can also send the output of dk.corpusmatch directly to dk.sampler~, bypassing its internal buffer~. This will also pass along any additional descriptor buffers required for loudness and spectral compensation.

method.name: read
method.description: Reads an audio file directly from a relative or absolute path. With no additional argument, a file dialog will appear to choose a file.

method.name: readfolder
method.description: Reads an audio folder directly from a relative or absolute path. With no additional argument, a file dialog will appear to choose a folder.

attribute.name: buffer
attribute.description: Polybuffer name to take when being used for non-corpus playback.

attribute.name: gain
attribute.description: Output gain of sample playback (in %).

attribute.name: in
attribute.description: Fade in amount (0-100%).

attribute.name: incurve
attribute.description: Curve for attack fade in (0% = linear, -100% = exponential, 100% = logarithmic).

attribute.name: length
attribute.description: Sample length to play (0-100%).

attribute.name: lengthabsolute
attribute.description: Sample length to play (in ms). This will be compared to the relative length and the lesser of the two will be taken as the actual length.

attribute.name: loop
attribute.description: Enables looping of playback. The loop parameter applies globally so if you have dk.corpusplayer~ set to 16 voices (the default) you can quickly end up with a chaotic and polymetric loop.

attribute.name: loudnesscomp
attribute.digest: Amount of loudness compensation to apply
attribute.description: Amount of loudness compensation to apply (bewteen -100 and 100%).

attribute.name: midinote
attribute.description: Sets default MIDI note when transposing.

attribute.name: out
attribute.description: Fade out amount (0-100%).

attribute.name: outcurve
attribute.description: Curve for fade out (0% = linear, -100% = logarithmic, 100% = exponential).

attribute.name: pitch
attribute.digest: Amount of half steps to transpose up or down (when in timestretch mode)
attribute.description: Amount of half steps to transpose up or down (when in timestretch mode) (-24-24).

attribute.name: spectralcomp
attribute.digest: Amount of spectral compensation to apply
attribute.description: Amount of spectral compensation to apply (bewteen -100 and 100%).

attribute.name: speed
attribute.description: Playback speed (-1000 to 1000%).

attribute.name: start
attribute.description: Sample start offset (0-50%).

attribute.name: startabsolute
attribute.description: Sample start offset (in ms). This will be added to the relative offset to combine the two.

attribute.name: steal
attribute.digest: Whether poly voice stealing is enabled
attribute.description: Sets whether voice stealing is enabled, which allows new samples to start when previous ones are still sounding.

attribute.name: timestretch
attribute.description: Enables timestretch mode.

attribute.name: timestretchmode
attribute.description: Timestretch algorithm to use (basic, monophonic, rhythmic, general, extremestretch, efficient).

attribute.name: voices
attribute.description: Sets the amount of polyphony available for sample playback.
